title: Foreign Medical Program Modernization Act of 2025
short_title: Foreign Medical Program Modernization Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes it easier for veterans living outside the United States to get medical care from the VA. Right now, veterans can only get this foreign medical care if they have a disability caused by their military service. The bill removes this rule so any veteran living abroad can get medical care. The VA will also have to update how it pays for this care and look into hiring companies to help find doctors and hospitals in other countries for veterans to use.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Health.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Foreign Medical Program Modernization Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Expansion of Foreign Medical Program of Department of Veterans Affairs
sections.text: This section makes changes to help more veterans get medical care when they are outside the United States. Right now, veterans can only get this care if they have a disability caused by their military service. This law removes that rule so any veteran can get medical care outside the country. The Department of Veterans Affairs must update their computer systems to pay doctors and hospitals using electronic money transfers instead of checks. The Department must also study whether it would be helpful to hire a company to build a network of hospitals and doctors around the world that could treat veterans. They need to figure out if this would make it easier or harder for veterans to get the care they need.
